metadata.notice: Every entry is a reviewed display placement of a remote source image. None is an accepted survey georegistration or an alignment-analysis target.

placements.title: Wavra Farm 1997 aerial landmark placement
placements.corner_basis: Exact projective conversion of the user-supplied Google Earth Pro GroundOverlay LatLonBox. The raw KML box and rotation are retained as the authoritative display geometry.
placements.site_text: The user matched the ICCRA aerial to the field south of the divided-highway corridor using the road and drive geometry, tree rows, farm boundaries, buildings, and curved access roads visible in the 2000 historical frame.

source_report_controls.reported_place: Wavra Farm, Salem / Silver Creek Falls, Marion County

placements.scale_basis: The user-authored KML is anisotropic: approximately 0.83736 m/px across the source x-axis and 1.81328 m/px down the source y-axis.
placements.orientation_basis: Google Earth Pro stores the image in a LatLonBox rotated 121.6502227783203 degrees counterclockwise; the derived source +x bearing is 328.3497772216797 degrees true.
placements.review_basis: The user supplied the actual Google Earth Pro registration and a historical-imagery screenshot. The previous Jordan Street placement is rejected: its anchor was 1,428 metres from the supplied formation anchor and did not match the highway, drives, tree rows, buildings, or field edges.
placements.quality_limitations: The registration is user-authored and has no independent withheld checkpoint set. The source image is oblique and the KML deliberately applies unequal horizontal and vertical scale.
placements.quality_disclosure: User-supplied Google Earth landmark registration. The raw KML geometry and historical-imagery date are preserved; the placement remains excluded from formal alignment calculations pending independent checkpoints.
placements.notes: The ICCRA aerial loads only after explicit user action. This record reproduces the supplied KMZ instead of the rejected Jordan Street placement.

placements.title: Rockville 1 2003 aerial landmark placement
placements.corner_basis: Three-point affine registration from non-collinear persistent road controls: western Rockville Road, the Rockville Road/Suisun Valley Road intersection, and Suisun Valley Road south of the intersection. The affine model solves rotation, scale, shear, and unequal axis scale for the oblique frame.
placements.reference_imagery: Current Esri World Imagery, checked against the source road and parcel scene
placements.site_text: The source scene is registered southwest of Rockville Road and Suisun Valley Road using three non-collinear road controls. The corrected footprint aligns the named intersection and both road approaches instead of forcing an unskewed two-point similarity fit.

controls.name: Rockville Road and Suisun Valley Road intersection

placements.review_basis: The named road scene and persistent intersection geometry constrain the correct field. Three non-collinear road controls replace the rejected two-point similarity fit and reproduce the visible shear of the oblique aerial frame.
placements.quality_limitations: The three controls solve the affine transform exactly, so no residual can be estimated from them. No independent withheld checkpoint or same-date orthophoto is available, and relief/perspective outside the road-control triangle can still distort the frame.
placements.quality_disclosure: Three-control affine road registration, not a survey georegistration. The roads and intersection are aligned, while the crop center and outer corners retain 75 m provisional uncertainty.
placements.notes: The source-hosted aerial loads only after explicit user action and remains excluded from formal alignment calculations.

placements.title: Rockville 2 2003 campus landmark placement
placements.corner_basis: Similarity transform from the source formation anchor and the persistent Solano Community College tennis-court control, published as four projective display corners for the oblique frame.
placements.reference_imagery: Current Esri World Imagery of Solano Community College
placements.site_text: The source aerial is matched to the Solano Community College field using the tennis courts, campus buildings, parking geometry, arterial road, and exact field corner.
placements.scale_basis: The formation anchor and tennis-court control produce a 1.480830976 m/px similarity scale.
placements.orientation_basis: The formation-to-tennis-court source vector was mapped to the corresponding WGS84 vector, yielding source +x at 263.938213 degrees true.
placements.review_basis: The distinctive campus, tennis courts, parking, road, and field geometry identify the scene. A two-control similarity seed supplies scale and rotation for the projective display footprint.
placements.quality_limitations: The tennis-court target was read manually from current imagery and may carry roughly 15-30 m error; the source frame is oblique and lacks an independent checkpoint.
placements.quality_disclosure: Campus landmark scene placement, not a survey georegistration. Multiple persistent features identify the field, but the precise four-corner warp remains provisional.
placements.notes: The actual ICCRA aerial frame loads only after explicit user action and remains excluded from formal alignment calculations.

placements.title: Miamisburg 2004 report-coordinate image placement
placements.corner_basis: The source frame was centered on the historical Google Earth coordinate recorded in the ICCRA investigation and oriented along the report's measured line toward Miamisburg Mound.
placements.reference_imagery: Historical Google Earth coordinate and alignment documented in the ICCRA investigation report
placements.site_text: The ICCRA investigation records the formation at 39 degrees 37 minutes 12.36 seconds north, 84 degrees 16 minutes 14.08 seconds west and documents its southeast position relative to Miamisburg Mound.
placements.scale_basis: A provisional 0.30 m/px display scale is consistent with the report's 220-foot formation length across the source frame.
placements.orientation_basis: The report-coordinate-to-mound relationship fixes the image y-down axis near 131.68 degrees true and the source x-right axis near 221.68 degrees true.
placements.review_basis: The investigation's degree-minute-second coordinate, named mound, measured formation length, and documented alignment provide a reproducible center and display orientation.
placements.quality_limitations: The transform is reconstructed from the report coordinate, length, and mound alignment rather than a withheld multi-point ground-control set.
placements.quality_disclosure: Report-coordinate image placement, not a survey georegistration. The center and named-landmark orientation are documented; the four image corners remain a provisional reconstruction.
placements.notes: The source-hosted formation image loads only after explicit user action and is excluded from formal alignment calculations pending independent checkpoints.

placements.title: Hopewell / Chillicothe 2012 landmark scene placement
placements.corner_basis: Manual four-point scene fit to the southern Hopewell earthwork boundary, Sulphur Lick corridor, North Fork Paint Creek, farm buildings, and the power-line corridor.
placements.reference_imagery: Current Esri imagery and NPS Hopewell Mound Group site geography
placements.site_text: The report distances place the formation south of the Hopewell enclosure, north of North Fork Paint Creek, and east of the power-line corridor; persistent field and farm landmarks constrain this scene.
placements.scale_basis: The oblique frame is anisotropic; the reported distances and persistent field span support the published four-corner fit rather than a uniform metric scale.
placements.orientation_basis: The scene was rotated to the creek, enclosure, road, farm, and power-line configuration; source x is approximately 145 degrees true near the formation.
placements.review_basis: Five persistent geographic controls and three report distances identify the correct field and support an explicitly provisional projective display footprint.
placements.quality_limitations: The source image is oblique, the formation anchor is approximate to about 15 pixels, and no independent withheld checkpoint set validates the four corners.
placements.quality_disclosure: Multi-landmark scene placement, not a surveyed registration. The field is well constrained by the report and terrain, while the precise four-corner warp remains provisional.
placements.notes: The source-hosted aerial loads only after explicit user action and remains excluded from formal alignment calculations.

placements.title: Albion / Starr Earthworks 2002 landmark scene placement
placements.corner_basis: Manual scene fit to the triangular Starr Commonwealth field bounded by 26 Mile Road, Interstate 94, and the persistent campus tree line.
placements.reference_imagery: Current Esri imagery and Starr Commonwealth campus geography
placements.site_text: The source aerial's triangular field, road edges, interstate corridor, and campus tree line match the Starr Commonwealth campus scene east of Albion.
placements.scale_basis: A provisional local scale near 3.7 m/px was estimated from the persistent triangular field and road-to-tree-line span.
placements.orientation_basis: The field and road geometry place source x near 103 degrees true; the four published corners retain the oblique scene perspective.
placements.review_basis: The named Starr venue and distinctive triangular field bounded by two persistent transport corridors identify the scene and support a provisional footprint.
placements.quality_limitations: The 2002 frame is low resolution and oblique; corner precision is limited and no independent withheld checkpoint set is available.
placements.quality_disclosure: Venue-field landmark placement, not a survey georegistration. The scene match is strong; precise corner geometry remains provisional.
placements.notes: The source-hosted image loads only after explicit user action and remains excluded from formal alignment calculations.

placements.title: Naperville 2002 Diehl Road field placement
placements.corner_basis: Exact three-control affine display transform from the 310 by 178 source frame to the official Illinois 2005 Chicago Urban Area orthophoto. The controls are the two ends of the Iroquois Club north parking boundary and the northern office-parking corner visible at the opposite side of the field.
placements.reference_imagery: Illinois State Geological Survey IL_CUA_2005 official orthophoto service
placements.site_text: The source report places the six-to-eight-acre soybean field off Diehl Road on Molex property and in the shadow of an apartment complex. The source photograph matches the surviving Iroquois Club building and north parking geometry, with corporate parking on the opposite side of the field.

source_report_controls.reported_context: apartment complex and corporate office buildings

controls.name: Iroquois Club north parking boundary east end
Iroquois Club north parking boundary west end

placements.review_basis: The full report, named Diehl Road and Molex property, documented apartment-complex context, field acreage, source photograph, and official 1998 and 2005 Illinois orthophotos identify the former field. Three non-collinear parking/field controls reproduce the source rotation, unequal scale, and shear.
placements.quality_limitations: The low-resolution source is strongly oblique. All three controls solve the affine transform, leaving no independent residual or held-out checkpoint; building height and outer corners may retain perspective error.
placements.quality_disclosure: Three-control affine field registration, not a survey georegistration. The correct former field is strongly supported, while the source footprint and transformed formation center retain 75 m provisional uncertainty.
placements.notes: The ICCRA-hosted source frame loads only after explicit user action and remains excluded from formal alignment calculations.

placements.title: Paint Creek Island 2003 creek-boundary placement
placements.corner_basis: Exact four-control projective display transform from the 275 by 184 ICCRA frame to the surviving Paint Creek island boundary. The controls are four non-collinear intersections of the persistent creek-side tree line and the enclosed field boundary.
placements.reference_imagery: Esri World Imagery current mosaic and NPS Seip Earthworks control
placements.site_text: The investigation places the formation on a tree-surrounded island in Paint Creek, at least one-half mile from a road and less than two miles from Seip Earthworks. The wide 2003 aerial independently matches Bainbridge, the creek loop, the island, and the Seip vicinity; the display frame matches the four persistent island boundaries.

source_report_controls.reported_landform: island in Paint Creek surrounded by a wide band of trees

visual_matches: creek channel immediately northwest of the island
Bainbridge town edge in the companion wide aerial
Seip Earthworks vicinity in the companion wide aerial

placements.review_basis: The ICCRA report, the 2003 wide aerial, NPS Seip coordinates, current imagery, and the distinctive surviving Paint Creek loop identify one field. Four non-collinear persistent island-boundary controls reproduce the oblique frame with a projective transform.
placements.quality_limitations: All four controls participate in the exact projective solution, leaving no independent held-out ground checkpoint. Tree growth, river migration, low source resolution, and extrapolation near the outer frame limit corner precision.
placements.quality_disclosure: Four-control creek-island projective placement, not a survey georegistration. The field identity is strongly supported; the footprint and transformed formation center retain 100 m provisional uncertainty.
placements.notes: The ICCRA-hosted source frame loads only after explicit user action and remains excluded from formal alignment calculations.

placements.title: Serpent Mound / Locust Grove 2003 road-aligned field placement
placements.corner_basis: Road-aligned anisotropic affine display fit from the 274 by 183 ICCRA frame. The visible Ohio 73 segment is matched at two separated points, while the source-frame formation anchor is placed within the uniquely resolved field shown by the ICCRA mapping slide.
placements.reference_imagery: Esri World Imagery current mosaic, ICCRA 2003 mapping aerial, and contemporaneous newspaper location description
placements.site_text: The formation was reported across from the Serpent Mound entrance on Ohio 73 and along Brush Creek. The ICCRA mapping slide shows the formation, road, creek, entrance, and mound in one frame; those controls resolve the field southwest of the entrance. The direct ICCRA aerial's visible road segment supports a provisional display fit in that field.

source_report_controls.reported_context: across from the entrance of Serpent Mound
source_report_controls.reported_diameter_range_ft: approximately 200 to 300 across the surviving reports

controls.name: formation center from ICCRA mapping aerial

visual_matches: field immediately southwest of the Serpent Mound entrance
Brush Creek crossing and tree corridor
direct formation-to-mound alignment shown in the ICCRA case PDF

placements.review_basis: The public ICCRA report and case PDF, the 2003 wide aerial, a contemporaneous newspaper location description, the persistent Ohio 73 and Brush Creek geometry, and current imagery identify one field. Two separated road points and the historically mapped formation anchor provide a bounded affine display fit.
placements.quality_limitations: The direct 274 by 183 frame is oblique and exposes only a short road segment. The formation anchor comes from the companion mapping aerial rather than a surveyed coordinate, the source reports disagree on overall diameter, and no independent held-out checkpoint validates scale or corners.
placements.quality_disclosure: Provisional road-aligned affine placement, not a survey georegistration. The unique field is well supported; the image footprint and formation center retain 100 m uncertainty and remain excluded from formal alignment calculations.
placements.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. The ICCRA-hosted source frame loads only after explicit user action and is not packaged by the atlas.

placements.title: Chesterton Windmill 2018 field placement
placements.corner_basis: Exact three-control affine display transform from the 800 by 306 source frame to an Esri World Imagery export. Controls are the publisher-mapped formation center, the persistent Chesterton Windmill building centroid, and the northern windmill-island crop-boundary intersection.
placements.reference_imagery: Esri World Imagery current mosaic, OpenStreetMap Chesterton Windmill building geometry, and the publisher map target
placements.site_text: The publisher coordinate places the formation in the field immediately northwest of Chesterton Windmill. The near-nadir source frame contains the formation, the windmill, and the distinctive persistent grass island surrounding the monument in one scene.

visual_matches: Chesterton Windmill tower and sail hub
distinctive hooked grass island surrounding the windmill
formation field immediately northwest of the monument
publisher coordinate at the formation center

placements.review_basis: The publisher map target identifies the formation center to 25 m, while the windmill building and its distinctive surrounding grass island persist in current imagery. Three non-collinear controls reproduce the source rotation, scale, and shear and place the complete near-nadir frame over the unique field.
placements.quality_limitations: All three controls participate in the exact affine solution, leaving no independent held-out checkpoint. Seasonal crop tracks differ between the 2018 source and current imagery, and the outer source-frame corners retain perspective and source-coordinate uncertainty.
placements.quality_disclosure: Three-control affine field registration, not a survey georegistration. The unique field and source orientation are strongly supported; the footprint remains provisional and excluded from formal alignment calculations.
placements.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

placements.title: Figsbury Ring 2016 hillfort-aligned field placement
placements.corner_basis: Exact four-control projective display transform from the 800 by 449 source frame. The publisher-mapped formation center and three distributed points on the persistent Figsbury Ring earthwork constrain the field, rotation, perspective, and scale.
placements.reference_imagery: Esri World Imagery current mosaic, the persistent Figsbury Ring earthwork, and the publisher map target
placements.site_text: The publisher coordinate places the formation in the arable field immediately southwest of Figsbury Ring. A wide source aerial contains the full formation and the complete persistent hillfort in one frame, uniquely resolving the field and scene orientation.

visual_matches: formation field immediately southwest of the hillfort
publisher coordinate at the formation center

placements.review_basis: The exact publisher map target identifies the formation center and the wide source aerial independently contains the complete persistent Figsbury Ring enclosure. Three distributed hillfort controls plus the formation anchor define a projective display fit; a held-out southern earthwork point provides an approximately 37 m residual check.
placements.quality_limitations: The source is oblique and the hillfort has real terrain relief, so one planar transform cannot eliminate parallax across the full frame. The controls were manually selected, the held-out earthwork checkpoint has a 37 m residual, and no surveyed ground control validates the corners.
placements.quality_disclosure: Four-control projective field registration, not a survey georegistration. The unique field and orientation are strongly supported; the full-frame footprint remains provisional and excluded from formal alignment calculations.
placements.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

placements.title: Yarnbury Castle 2018 earthwork-aligned field placement
placements.corner_basis: Exact four-control projective display transform from the 800 by 1139 source frame. The publisher-mapped formation center and three distributed points on the persistent Yarnbury Castle earthwork constrain the unique field, scene rotation, perspective, and scale.
placements.reference_imagery: Esri World Imagery current mosaic, the persistent Yarnbury Castle earthwork and A303, and the publisher map target
placements.site_text: The publisher coordinate places the formation in the arable field southeast of Yarnbury Castle and south of the A303. The selected source aerial contains the complete formation, A303, and the full persistent earthwork in one frame, uniquely resolving the field and orientation.

visual_matches: A303 alignment between the earthwork and formation
formation field southeast of the hillfort
publisher coordinate at the formation center

placements.review_basis: The exact publisher map target identifies the formation center and the wide source aerial independently contains Yarnbury Castle and the A303. Three distributed earthwork controls plus the formation anchor define a projective display fit; a held-out southeastern earthwork point has an approximately 13 m residual and the transformed road follows the reference A303.
placements.quality_limitations: The source is oblique and the earthwork has terrain relief, so a planar transform cannot eliminate all parallax across the frame. Controls were manually selected and no surveyed ground control validates the footprint corners.
placements.quality_disclosure: Four-control projective field registration, not a survey georegistration. The unique field, earthwork, and road orientation are strongly supported; the full-frame footprint remains provisional and excluded from formal alignment calculations.
placements.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

placements.title: Coneybury Hill 2018 documented-size crop-geometry placement
placements.corner_basis: Four-point projective crop-geometry rectification of the tight 800 by 501 aerial. The publisher map target fixes the center, the source report documents a 40 metre diameter, and the outer-ring cardinal points constrain local scale and perspective. Same-flight wide aerials showing the paired copses and field context constrain scene orientation, but do not supply independent ground control for this tight frame.
placements.reference_imagery: Publisher Streetmap target, documented 40 metre diameter, same-flight wide aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
placements.site_text: The exact publisher target places the 2018 Coneybury Hill formation in the arable field south of the A303. Same-flight wide aerials show the formation field, the distinctive paired copses, the A303 corridor, and the surrounding field pattern, resolving the unique field while the selected tight aerial preserves the formation geometry at useful resolution.
placements.review_basis: The publisher supplies a 25 metre coordinate target and reports an approximately 40 metre diameter. Four visible outer-ring cardinal points define a tight projective crop-geometry rectification around that target. Same-flight wide frames and the paired copses support the selected orientation and unique field, but the tight source frame contains no independent persistent ground checkpoint.
placements.quality_limitations: This is a crop-geometry placement, not a landmark or survey georegistration. The center inherits the publisher's 25 metre uncertainty; the 40 metre size is reported rather than surveyed; orientation is inferred from same-flight scene context and field tramlines; no persistent ground point independently validates the projected corners.
placements.quality_disclosure: Documented-size crop-geometry rectification, not a real-world accuracy benchmark or survey georegistration. It is useful for display and visual comparison only and remains excluded from alignment calculations.
placements.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide aerials place the two persistent copses on the source horizontal axis; the tight frame's tramline direction is consistent with that scene orientation.

placements.title: Cley Hill 2016 documented-size crop-geometry placement
placements.corner_basis: Four-point projective crop-geometry rectification of the tight 800 by 542 aerial. The publisher map target fixes the center, the source report documents a 43 metre diameter, and four outer-ring controls constrain local scale and perspective. Same-flight wide aerials identify the field immediately south of Cley Hill; the tight frame's tramlines align with the persistent field direction visible in reference imagery.
placements.reference_imagery: Publisher Streetmap target, documented 43 metre diameter, same-flight wide aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
placements.site_text: The exact publisher target places the 2016 formation in the arable field immediately south of Cley Hill. Same-flight wide aerials show the hill earthworks, the hill-to-field boundary, and the formation field, resolving the unique field; the selected tight aerial preserves the formation geometry and tramline direction at useful resolution.
placements.review_basis: The publisher supplies a 25 metre coordinate target and reports an approximately 43 metre diameter. Four visible outer-ring controls define a tight projective crop-geometry rectification around that target. Same-flight wide frames, Cley Hill, the field boundary, and the matching tramline direction support the selected field and orientation, but the tight source frame contains no independent persistent ground checkpoint.
placements.quality_limitations: This is a crop-geometry placement, not a landmark or survey georegistration. The center inherits the publisher's 25 metre uncertainty; the 43 metre size is reported rather than surveyed; orientation is inferred from same-flight context and matching field tramlines; no persistent ground point independently validates the projected corners.
placements.quality_disclosure: Documented-size crop-geometry rectification, not a real-world accuracy benchmark or survey georegistration. It is useful for display and visual comparison only and remains excluded from alignment calculations.
placements.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide aerials resolve the formation field immediately south of Cley Hill. The tight frame's tramline axis is aligned to the matching persistent southeast field direction in the reference mosaic.

placements.title: Cocum Farm 2019 documented-size crop-geometry placement
placements.corner_basis: Four-point projective crop-geometry rectification of the tight 800 by 500 aerial. The publisher map target fixes the center, the source report documents a 61 metre diameter, and four outer-ring controls constrain local scale and perspective. Same-flight wide aerials show the distinctive north-south tree avenue, adjoining hedgerows, road, and farm context, resolving the field and image-up orientation.
placements.reference_imagery: Publisher Streetmap target, documented 61 metre diameter, same-flight wide aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
placements.site_text: The publisher target places the 2019 formation in the arable field immediately south of Cocum Farm's long straight tree avenue. Same-flight wide aerials reproduce the avenue, its southern hedge junction, surrounding field boundaries, road, and farm cluster, resolving the unique field and yaw quadrant; the selected tight frame preserves the formation geometry.

source_report_controls.reported_place: Cocum Farm, near Barton Stacey, Hampshire

placements.review_basis: The publisher supplies a 25 metre coordinate target and reports an approximately 61 metre diameter. Four visible outer-ring controls define a tight projective crop-geometry rectification. Same-flight wide frames and the persistent tree avenue, hedge junction, road, and farm context identify the field and yaw quadrant, but the tight source frame contains no independent persistent ground checkpoint.
placements.quality_limitations: This is a crop-geometry placement, not a landmark or survey georegistration. The center inherits the publisher's 25 metre uncertainty; the 61 metre size is reported rather than surveyed; orientation is transferred from same-flight context; no persistent ground point independently validates the tight-frame corners.
placements.quality_disclosure: Documented-size crop-geometry rectification, not a real-world accuracy benchmark or survey georegistration. It is useful for display and visual comparison only and remains excluded from alignment calculations.
placements.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: The same-flight wide aerial places the long Cocum Farm tree avenue directly north of the formation and preserves the tight frame's star and tramline orientation, fixing image-up approximately to true north.

placements.title: Kitt's Lane 2021 documented-size crop-geometry placement
placements.corner_basis: Four-point projective crop-geometry rectification of the near-nadir 800 by 800 aerial. The publisher's BNG map target fixes the center, the source report documents a 55 metre diameter, and four outer-ring controls constrain local scale and perspective. Same-flight wide aerials show the field beside the persistent woodland block, Kitt's Lane buildings, hedges, and field corners, resolving the unique field and yaw quadrant.
placements.reference_imagery: Publisher BNG Streetmap target, documented 55 metre diameter, same-flight wide aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
placements.site_text: The publisher's BNG target places the 2021 formation in the arable field immediately east of the large Kitt's Lane woodland block. Same-flight wide aerials reproduce the woodland edge, nearby buildings, field corners, and hedge pattern, resolving the unique field; the selected near-nadir frame preserves the formation geometry.

source_report_controls.reported_place: Kitt's Lane, near West Meon, Hampshire

placements.review_basis: The publisher supplies a BNG target with 25 metre atlas uncertainty and reports an approximately 55 metre diameter. Four visible outer-ring controls define a tight projective crop-geometry rectification. Same-flight wide frames and persistent woodland, buildings, hedges, and field corners identify the field and yaw quadrant, but the tight source frame contains no independent persistent ground checkpoint.
placements.quality_limitations: This is a crop-geometry placement, not a landmark or survey georegistration. The center uses the transformed BNG coordinate; the Streetmap URL's human-readable latitude/longitude label differs from that BNG target. The 55 metre size is reported rather than surveyed, orientation is transferred from same-flight context, and no persistent ground point independently validates the tight-frame corners.
placements.quality_disclosure: Documented-size crop-geometry rectification, not a real-world accuracy benchmark or survey georegistration. It is useful for display and visual comparison only and remains excluded from alignment calculations.
placements.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide aerials match the woodland block, Kitt's Lane building cluster, hedgerows, and field corners; the near-nadir frame is rotated so image-up points approximately west-northwest toward that woodland context.
